element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Customizable button configuration allows player familiarity
Conventional format with standard command inputs
Multiple transformations per character; some unlockable via cheat codes
Directional dash system integrated into core movement
On-screen radar maintains spatial awareness during extended combat range

typical_tension_triggers: Opponent closing distance for combo attacks
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: One-on-one match pacing driven by player aggression and opponent AI response; cyclical tension builds through proximity-based combat phases

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Cheat code unlockable transformations suggest hidden depth mechanics

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tournament mode introduces basic one-on-one matchups with early-roster characters (Piccolo, Vegeta)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive opponent tier increase; introduction of Android and Cell saga characters with more complex movesets
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final opponents feature advanced transformations and AI aggression; Cell and higher-tier fighters demand mastery of special moves and positioning
notable_spikes: Introduction of split-screen mechanic during mid-range combat
Character transformations increase opponent damage output and move variety
AI difficulty scaling across tournament progression

notable_level_patterns.description: Game progresses through sequential one-on-one fights against roster characters in canonical Dragon Ball Z saga order (Piccolo → Vegeta → Frieza → Androids → Cell)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Battle Stage 3 (Frieza theme) represents mid-tournament progression point

notable_level_patterns.description: Each opponent occupies themed stage corresponding to their narrative arc; music and setting reinforce character identity and series progression
Split-screen mechanic activates when fighters exceed close-range proximity, forcing players to manage screen space and spatial awareness as new skill layer
